title: aneilema gramineum var. affine
intro: Aneilema gramineum var. affine, also known as the small green vine or twinflower, is a perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. It belongs to the family Commelinaceae, and its leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is known to contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as treat infections. Aneilema gramineum var. affine can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
